questions.title: Base Case
questions.description: Drag the correct tokens to complete the base case:
questions.explanation: Factorial stops when n <= 1.

questions.title: Complete Power Function (Recursive)
questions.description: Drag tokens to complete recursive power function:
questions.explanation: Recursive power: base case is exp == 0 returns 1, recursive case is base * power(base, exp-1).
hints: Base case: any number to power 0 is 1

questions.title: Recursion Basics
questions.description: Fill in the blanks about recursion:

questions.text: The stopping condition is called the ___ case

questions.explanation: Recursion requires a base case and progress toward it, here by subtracting 1.

questions.title: Recursion Terminology
questions.description: Fill in the blanks about recursion:

questions.text: The stopping condition is the ___ case

questions.explanation: Recursion needs a base case and a step that moves toward it.

questions.title: Rekursions Koncepter (Danish)

questions.text: ___ forhindrer uendelig rekursion ved at stoppe de rekursive kald
Når en funktion kalder sig selv, kaldes det ___
Hvert rekursivt kald opretter en ny ___ på stakken
For mange rekursive kald kan forårsage ___

questions.explanation: Rekursion: funktion kalder sig selv. Basistilfælde stopper rekursion. Hvert kald opretter stakramme. For dybt forårsager stak overflow.

questions.title: Recursion Concepts
questions.description: Fill in the blanks about recursion:

questions.text: The ___ prevents infinite recursion by stopping the recursive calls
When a function calls itself, it is called ___
Each recursive call creates a new ___ on the stack
Too many recursive calls can cause ___

questions.explanation: Recursion: function calls itself. Base case stops recursion. Each call creates stack frame. Too deep causes stack overflow.
hints: Base case is the stopping condition

questions.title: Basic Bitwise OR
questions.explanation: 12 in binary is 1100, 10 in binary is 1010. Bitwise OR: 1100 | 1010 = 1110 = 14.
hints: OR returns 1 when at least one bit is 1
Combine the bits where either has a 1

questions.title: Bitwise XOR Properties
questions.explanation: Any number XORed with itself equals 0 because XOR returns 1 only when bits differ. Since all bits are identical, all result bits are 0.
hints: XOR returns 1 when bits are different
What happens when you compare identical bits?
This is a useful property for clearing values

questions.title: Two's Complement Negation
questions.explanation: In two's complement, -x = ~x + 1. Here: ~5 = -6, then -6 + 1 = -5. This is how negation works at the bit level.
hints: Two's complement negation: invert all bits and add 1
This is how computers represent negative numbers

questions.title: Signed Integer Overflow
questions.description: What is the behavior of this code?
questions.explanation: Signed integer overflow is undefined behavior in C. In practice, on two's complement systems, it typically wraps to INT_MIN (-2147483648), but the standard doesn't guarantee this.
hints: Signed overflow is undefined by the C standard
Most systems use two's complement wrapping

questions.title: Factorial Calculation
questions.explanation: factorial(4) = 4 * factorial(3) = 4 * 3 * factorial(2) = 4 * 3 * 2 * factorial(1) = 4 * 3 * 2 * 1 * factorial(0) = 4 * 3 * 2 * 1 * 1 = 24

questions.title: Sum of Digits
questions.explanation: sum_digits(742) = 2 + sum_digits(74) = 2 + (4 + sum_digits(7)) = 2 + 4 + 7 = 13.
hints: Split the last digit with n % 10

questions.title: Missing Base Case
questions.description: What happens when this code runs?
questions.explanation: There is no base case. f keeps calling itself with decreasing n until the stack overflows.
hints: Recursion must have a base case
The stack grows until it crashes

questions.title: Sum to N
Print Before Call
questions.explanation: The print happens before the recursive call, so it prints 3, then 2, then 1.

questions.title: Print After Call
questions.explanation: The print happens after the recursive call, so it prints 1, then 2, then 3.

questions.title: Power Function
Recursive GCD
questions.explanation: gcd(48,18) is 6 using Euclid's algorithm.

questions.title: Count Digits
questions.explanation: 1000 has 4 digits. The function counts one digit per division by 10.

questions.title: Wrong Base Case
questions.description: What happens when this program runs?
questions.explanation: fact(0) never reaches the base case n == 1, so it recurses indefinitely until the stack overflows.
hints: Base case does not handle n == 0

questions.title: Sum Array Recursively
questions.explanation: The function adds 1 + 2 + 3 = 6.

questions.title: Add Two Recursively
questions.explanation: add_two(3) adds 2 three times: 2 + 2 + 2 = 6.

questions.title: Fibonacci Value
questions.explanation: Fibonacci sequence: 0,1,1,2,3,5,8. fib(6) is 8.

questions.title: Return Count
questions.explanation: The function adds 1 four times, so it returns 4.

questions.title: Step of Two
hints: Count how many calls until n <= 0

questions.title: Base Case
questions.description: What is the purpose of a base case in recursion?
questions.explanation: Base case is the stopping condition that prevents infinite recursion.

questions.title: Stack Depth
questions.description: What happens with very deep recursion?
questions.explanation: Each recursive call uses stack space. Too many calls cause stack overflow.

questions.title: Tail Recursion
questions.explanation: Option A is tail-recursive (recursive call is last operation). Option B adds after recursion.
hints: Tail recursion = recursive call is last thing
Option A: nothing after recursive call
Option B: addition after recursive call

questions.title: Mutual Recursion
questions.explanation: Mutual recursion: is_even calls is_odd, is_odd calls is_even. is_even(4) → is_odd(3) → is_even(2) → is_odd(1) → is_even(0) → 1.

questions.title: Recursion vs Iteration
questions.description: What is an advantage of recursion over iteration?
questions.explanation: Recursion is often more natural and elegant for tree, graph, and divide-and-conquer problems. But uses more memory.

questions.title: Recursive String Reverse
questions.explanation: Prints from last char to first: c, b, a. Output is "cba".

questions.title: Recursive Array Search
questions.explanation: Recursively searches array for target. Finds 5, returns 1 (true).

questions.title: Euclid's GCD Algorithm
questions.explanation: Euclid's algorithm finds greatest common divisor using modulo and recursion.

questions.title: Count Up
questions.explanation: The recursive call happens before printing, so it prints 1,2,3.

questions.title: Count Down
questions.explanation: The function prints before the recursive call, so it prints 3,2,1.

questions.title: Recursive Sum of Squares
Recursive Reverse Print
questions.explanation: Each call prints the last digit, then recurses on the rest. 123 prints 3,2,1.

questions.title: Power Base Case
questions.explanation: Any number to the power of 0 is 1, and the base case returns 1.

questions.title: Tail Recursion
questions.description: Which statement is TRUE about this function?
questions.explanation: The recursive call is the last operation, so it is tail-recursive.

questions.title: Factorial Base Case
questions.explanation: The base case returns 1 when n <= 1.

questions.title: Recursive Sum of Array
Recursive Fibonacci
questions.explanation: Fibonacci sequence: 0,1,1,2,3,5. fib(5) = 5.

questions.title: Recursive Count Digits
questions.explanation: 999 has 3 digits.

questions.title: Missing Base Case
questions.explanation: There is no base case, so it recurses forever until the stack overflows.

questions.title: Sum to N
Recursive Min
questions.explanation: min2 returns the smaller value: 3.

questions.title: Recursive String Length
questions.explanation: The length of "hey" is 3.

questions.title: Mutual Recursion
questions.explanation: 4 is even, so is_even(4) returns 1.

questions.title: Binary Search Recursion
questions.explanation: The function returns the index if found, otherwise -1.

questions.title: Recursive Max
questions.explanation: max2 returns the larger value: 9.

questions.title: Tower of Hanoi Moves
questions.explanation: Tower of Hanoi with 3 disks requires 2^3 - 1 = 7 moves. hanoi_moves(3) = 2*hanoi_moves(2) + 1 = 2*(2*hanoi_moves(1) + 1) + 1 = 2*(2*1 + 1) + 1 = 2*3 + 1 = 7.

questions.title: Fibonacci with Memoization
questions.description: What is the time complexity improvement of this memoized version?
options: From O(n^2) to O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Regular recursive Fibonacci is O(2^n) due to repeated calculations. Memoization stores results, reducing it to O(n) as each fib(i) is computed only once.

questions.title: Merge Sort Partition
questions.description: What does this helper function do in merge sort?
options: Divides array and recursively sorts halves
questions.explanation: This is the classic divide-and-conquer merge sort: divide array in half, recursively sort each half, then merge the sorted halves.

questions.title: Quick Sort Partition
questions.description: What is the purpose of the recursive calls in quick sort?
options: Sort elements smaller and larger than pivot
questions.explanation: Quick sort partitions array around pivot, then recursively sorts elements smaller than pivot (left) and larger than pivot (right).

questions.title: N-Queens Backtracking
questions.explanation: This is classic backtracking: try placing queen, recurse; if it fails, undo (backtrack) and try next position.

questions.title: Subset Sum Backtracking
options: If subset exists with given sum
questions.explanation: This checks if any subset of array elements sums to the target sum, using recursive backtracking to try including/excluding each element.

questions.title: Tail Recursion Conversion
questions.description: Which version can be optimized by compiler to avoid stack growth?
questions.explanation: Version A is tail recursive (recursive call is last operation), allowing tail call optimization to reuse stack frame. Version B performs addition after recursion, preventing optimization.
hints: Tail recursion = last operation is recursive call
Version A has nothing after recursion

questions.title: Factorial Tail Recursive
questions.explanation: This is tail-recursive factorial with accumulator. fact_tail(5,1) = fact_tail(4,5) = fact_tail(3,20) = fact_tail(2,60) = fact_tail(1,120) = 120.

questions.title: Converting to Iteration
questions.description: Which iterative version is equivalent to this recursion?
options: int s=0; for(int i=n; i>0; i--) s+=i;
int s=1; for(int i=1; i<=n; i++) s*=i;
int s=n; for(int i=0; i<n; i++) s--;
int s=0; for(int i=0; i<n; i++) s=i;
questions.explanation: The recursive function sums from n down to 1. The equivalent iteration is: s=0; for(i=n; i>0; i--) s+=i;

questions.title: Stack Depth Limit
questions.description: What happens when recursion depth exceeds system limits?
questions.explanation: Each recursive call consumes stack space. With 100,000 calls, the stack will overflow, causing segmentation fault or stack overflow error.

questions.title: Palindrome Check Recursion
questions.explanation: "racecar" is a palindrome. The function compares characters from both ends moving inward, returns 1 (true) for palindrome.

questions.title: Ackermann Function
questions.explanation: Ackermann(2,2) = Ackermann(1, Ackermann(2,1)) = Ackermann(1, Ackermann(1, Ackermann(2,0))) = Ackermann(1, Ackermann(1, Ackermann(1,1))) = ... = 7.

questions.title: Binary Search Tree Insert
questions.explanation: Recursively inserts value into BST: if empty, create node; if value < root, insert left; if value >= root, insert right.

questions.title: Fibonacci Base Cases
questions.explanation: Base case: if n <= 1 return n. So fib(0) returns 0.

questions.title: Memoization Array Setup
questions.description: What should memo array be initialized to for memoization?
questions.explanation: Memoization arrays should be initialized to a sentinel value (typically -1) that cannot be a valid result, to distinguish computed from uncomputed values.

questions.title: Permutation Generation
questions.description: What does this recursive pattern generate?
questions.explanation: This generates all permutations using backtracking: swap elements, recurse, then backtrack (undo swap) to try next permutation.

questions.title: Mutual Recursion Even/Odd
questions.explanation: 5 is odd, so is_odd(5) should return 1. Trace: is_odd(5) -> is_even(4) -> is_odd(3) -> is_even(2) -> is_odd(1) -> is_even(0) -> 1.

questions.title: Recursion to Iteration Fibonacci
questions.description: Which iterative code is equivalent to recursive Fibonacci?
options: int a=0,b=1; for(int i=2;i<=n;i++){int t=a+b;a=b;b=t;} return b;
int s=0; for(int i=0;i<n;i++) s+=i; return s;
int p=1; for(int i=1;i<=n;i++) p*=i; return p;
int a=n; while(n>0) n--; return a;
questions.explanation: Iterative Fibonacci maintains two previous values (a, b) and computes next as their sum, similar to the recurrence fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2).
hints: Update a and b each iteration

questions.title: Maze Solving Backtracking
questions.description: What strategy does this maze solver use?
questions.explanation: This uses backtracking: mark cell as part of path, try moving right/down, if both fail, unmark cell (backtrack) and return false.

questions.title: Trace Recursive Function f(x)
questions.description: Trace the execution of this recursive function and determine the output for each test case:
questions.explanation: This function recursively computes a value by adding 2*x at each level until reaching the base case (x < 0). The base case returns -1, then each level adds 2*x as it unwinds.
hints: Start with the base case: what happens when x < 0?
For each recursive call, x decreases by 1
The function adds 2*x as it returns from each level

questions.title: Trace Factorial Function
questions.explanation: Factorial is a classic recursive function. It multiplies n by the factorial of (n-1) until reaching the base case where n <= 1, which returns 1.

questions.title: Trace Fibonacci Function
questions.explanation: Fibonacci recursively computes by adding the two previous numbers. Base cases are fib(0)=0 and fib(1)=1. Note how fib(2) is calculated twice - this is inefficient but demonstrates the recursive structure.
hints: The function branches into two recursive calls at each step

questions.title: Trace Sum to N
questions.description: Trace the execution of this recursive sum function:
questions.explanation: The function adds n to the sum of numbers below it until reaching 0. It accumulates the total as the recursion unwinds.
hints: Each call adds n and decreases by 1

questions.title: Trace Sum Function
questions.description: Trace the execution of this recursive sum function:
questions.explanation: This function sums numbers from 1 to n. Base case: sum_to_n(0) = 0. Recursive: n + sum_to_n(n-1).
hints: Each level adds n to the sum of smaller numbers

questions.title: Trace Power of Two
questions.description: Trace the execution of this power function:
questions.explanation: pow2 computes 2^n by multiplying by 2 on each recursive step until it reaches n = 0.

questions.title: Trace GCD Function
questions.description: Trace the Greatest Common Divisor function:
questions.explanation: Euclidean algorithm for GCD. Base case: if b is 0, return a. Recursive: gcd(b, a mod b).
hints: Base case: when b is 0, a is the GCD
Recursive case swaps parameters: gcd(b, a % b)
Each step reduces the problem size

questions.title: Trace Double
questions.description: Trace the execution of this function:
questions.explanation: The function adds 2 for each step down to 0.

questions.title: Trace Ackermann Function (Limited)
questions.description: Trace a simplified version of the Ackermann function:
questions.explanation: Ackermann function has multiple base cases and nested recursion. Grows extremely fast.
hints: Three cases: m==0, n==0, or nested recursion
Very deep recursion for larger inputs

questions.title: Trace Power
questions.description: Trace the execution of this power function:
questions.explanation: The function multiplies base by itself exp times until exp reaches 0.

questions.title: Trace String Reverse
questions.explanation: Recursive string reversal: recurse to end, then print characters as stack unwinds.

questions.title: Trace Digit Sum
questions.description: Trace the execution of this sum of digits function:
questions.explanation: The function splits the last digit using % 10 and recurses on n / 10.

questions.title: Trace Power Function
questions.explanation: Recursive power: base case is exp == 0 returns 1. Recursive: base * power(base, exp-1).

questions.title: Trace Count Digits
questions.description: Trace the execution of this digit-counting function:
questions.explanation: Each call removes one digit by dividing by 10 and adds 1 to the count.

questions.title: Trace Digit Sum
questions.explanation: Sum of digits: base case is single digit. Recursive: last digit + sum of remaining digits.
hints: Base case: single digit returns itself

questions.title: Trace Multiply
questions.description: Trace the execution of this multiply function:
questions.explanation: Multiplication is repeated addition until b reaches 0.

questions.title: Trace Binary Search
questions.explanation: Binary search: divide array in half, compare middle element, search left or right half recursively.
hints: Base case: left > right means not found
Search left half if target smaller, right half if larger

questions.title: Trace GCD
questions.description: Trace the execution of this gcd function:
questions.explanation: Euclid's algorithm recurses with (b, a % b) until b becomes 0.

questions.title: Trace Count Digits
questions.explanation: Count digits: base case is single digit. Recursive: 1 + count_digits(n/10).
hints: Divide by 10 to remove last digit

questions.title: Trace Sum Array
questions.description: Trace the execution of this array sum function:
questions.explanation: The function adds elements from the end toward the start until n reaches 0.

questions.title: Trace Is Even/Odd
questions.explanation: Check even by subtracting 2 until reaching 0 (even) or 1 (odd). Two base cases.

questions.title: Trace is_even
questions.description: Trace the execution of this parity check:
questions.explanation: The function subtracts 2 until it reaches 0 (even) or 1 (odd).

questions.title: Trace Factorial 4
questions.description: Trace the execution of this factorial function:
questions.explanation: Factorial multiplies n by factorial(n-1) until n <= 1.

questions.title: Trace Sum of Odds
questions.description: Trace the execution of this recursive function:
questions.explanation: The function adds 1 for each odd number from 1 to n.

questions.title: Trace Sum to N
questions.description: Trace the execution of this sum function:
questions.explanation: The function adds n down to 0.

questions.title: Trace String Length
questions.description: Trace the execution of this recursive string length function:
questions.explanation: The function counts characters until it reaches the null terminator.

questions.title: Trace Power
questions.description: Trace the execution of this power function:
questions.explanation: Each call multiplies by the base until exp reaches 0.

questions.title: Trace Count Digits
questions.description: Trace the execution of this digit counter:
questions.explanation: Each call removes one digit by dividing by 10.

questions.title: Trace Sum of Digits
questions.description: Trace the execution of this sum of digits function:
questions.explanation: Use modulo to peel off digits and recurse on n/10.

questions.title: Trace Fibonacci 3
questions.description: Trace the execution of this Fibonacci function:
questions.explanation: Fibonacci adds the two previous numbers.

questions.title: Trace GCD
questions.description: Trace the execution of this gcd function:
questions.explanation: Euclid's algorithm uses repeated modulo until b is 0.

questions.title: Trace Multiply
questions.description: Trace the execution of this multiply function:
questions.explanation: Multiplication is repeated addition until b is 0.

questions.title: Trace Sum Array
questions.description: Trace the execution of this array sum function:
questions.explanation: The function adds elements from the end toward the start.

questions.title: Trace String Length
questions.description: Trace the execution of this string length function:
questions.explanation: The function counts characters until it reaches '\0'.

questions.title: Trace Sum Even Count
questions.description: Trace the execution of this even counter:
questions.explanation: Adds 1 for even numbers and 0 for odd numbers.
hints: Count of evens up to 4 is 2

questions.title: Trace Count Down
questions.description: Trace the execution of this countdown function:
questions.explanation: Each call adds 1 until n reaches 0.

questions.title: Trace Min of Two
questions.description: Trace the execution of this min function:
questions.explanation: The function returns the smaller of the two values.

questions.title: Trace Reverse Digits
questions.description: Trace the execution of this digit printer:
questions.explanation: Each call prints the last digit then recurses on the remaining number.

questions.title: Trace Binary Search
questions.description: Trace the execution of this binary search:
questions.explanation: Binary search halves the range until it finds the target or fails.
